q: An organic compound A upon reacting with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>NH</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math><sub> </sub>gives B. On heating, B gives C. C in presence of KOH reacts with Br<sub>2</sub> to give CH<sub>3</sub>CH<sub>2</sub>NH<sub>2</sub>. A is :
question: An organic compound A upon reacting with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>NH</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math><sub> </sub>gives B. On heating, B gives C. C in presence of KOH reacts with Br<sub>2</sub> to give CH<sub>3</sub>CH<sub>2</sub>NH<sub>2</sub>. A is :
solution:
Ammonia reacts with carboxylic acid to form ammonium carboxylate (B). Which on heating form amide (C). Amide on reaction with bromine and KOH undergo Hoffmann Bromamide reaction to form amine with one carbon less.

CH3−CH2−CO||−OH+NH3(A)→CH3CH2−CO||−O⊖(B)N⊕H4CH3CH2−CO||−O⊖(B)N⊕H4→ΔCH3CH2−CO||−NH2(C)CH3CH2−CO||−NH2(C)→(Hoffmann bromamide degradation)Br2/KOHCH3CH2−NH2

question:
Given below are two statements :

Statement I: The esterification of carboxylic acid with an alcohol is a nucleophilic acyl substitution.

Statement II: Electron withdrawing groups in the carboxylic acid will increase the rate of esterification reaction.

Choose the most appropriate option
options: Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect.
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect.
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct.
Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct.
solution: In the above reaction OH group is replaced by OR group. Hence, nucleophilic substitution reaction is involved. Electron withdrawing groups increases the stability of RC⊕OH2, hence, rate of reaction increases.

q: Decarboxylation of all six possible forms of diaminobenzoic acids <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mn>6</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><msub><mfenced><msub><mi>NH</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msub><mi>COOH</mi></math> yields three products <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math>. Three acids give a product '<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math>', two acids gives a product '<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></math>' and one acid give a product '<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math>'. The melting point of product '<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math>' is
question: Decarboxylation of all six possible forms of diaminobenzoic acids <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mn>6</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><msub><mfenced><msub><mi>NH</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msub><mi>COOH</mi></math> yields three products <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math>. Three acids give a product '<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math>', two acids gives a product '<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></math>' and one acid give a product '<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math>'. The melting point of product '<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math>' is
solution:
C is a para isomer.

The melting point of para-isomer is comparatively higher than the corresponding  ortho and meta-isomers , because of more symmetrical nature; it fits into crystal lattice more readily and so, close packing of molecules in crystal lattice increases the intermolecular force of attraction between molecules.

Hence C has highest melting point that is 142 °C

question: Ethyl isocyanide on hydrolysis in acidic medium generates

question:
Major product 'P' formed in the following reaction is 

 Major product
solution: In the first step bromine attacks the carbon-carbon double bond by electrophilic addition reaction mechanism and forms the bromonium cyclic intermediate. In the presence of sodium bicarbonate carboxylic acid group present in the reactant converts to carboxylate ion. This carboxylate ion opens the cyclic bromonium ion ring and forms the desired product.

Rate of hydrolysis <math><mi>α</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>M</mi></math>  effect or <math><mo>-</mo><mi>I</mi></math> of ester. <math><mo>-</mo><mi>M</mi><mo>></mo><mo>-</mo><mi>I</mi></math>. <math><mo>-</mo><mi>N</mi><msub><mrow><mi>O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math>  is a strong <math><mi>E</mi><mi>W</mi><mi>G</mi></math> (Electron withdrawing group),<math><mo>-</mo><mi>C</mi><mi>l</mi></math> is a weak <math><mi>E</mi><mi>W</mi><mi>G</mi></math> and <math><mi>O</mi><mi>C</mi><msub><mrow><mi>H</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub></math> is an <math><mi>E</mi><mi>D</mi><mi>G</mi></math>(Electron donating group).
